_README: Prototype KPI dataset for the Reach Riverside logic model. Keyed by EXACT outcome label (must match the text rendered in the Short-term / Intermediate / Long-term Outcomes columns). Each outcome maps to an array of indicator objects. 'direction' = 'up' if higher is better, 'down' if lower is better (e.g. evictions). 'tier' = Core | Secondary (used by the priority filter). 'status' is explicit here for demo clarity; the layer will also derive a status from current vs. target if status is omitted. SAMPLE/ILLUSTRATIVE VALUES — not official Reach figures.

Increased access to affordable rental ho.indicator: Affordable rental units available to neighbors
Increased access to affordable rental ho.definition: Count of income-restricted rental units available in the Riverside footprint.

Increased access to affordable rental ho.indicator: Median rent-to-income ratio for placed households
Increased access to affordable rental ho.definition: Share of household income spent on rent for newly housed neighbors.

Increased access to wealth creation oppo.definition: Unduplicated residents enrolled in homeownership, savings, or business programs.

Increased acreage RESTORED.definition: Cumulative acreage brought back into productive use.

Increased access to purchasing permanent.indicator: Community Land Trust homes available for purchase
Increased access to purchasing permanent.definition: Permanently-affordable for-sale homes in the CLT inventory.

Increased access to rental, utility, and.definition: Households that received at least one assistance disbursement.

Increased student attendance.definition: Share of students missing 10%+ of school days.

Increased math and literacy levels.indicator: Students at/above grade level in literacy
Increased math and literacy levels.definition: Share of students meeting grade-level literacy benchmark.

Increased access to affordable, high qua.indicator: Neighbors with a usual source of care
Increased access to affordable, high qua.definition: Share of surveyed residents reporting a regular healthcare provider.

Increased access to job skills training.definition: Unduplicated residents enrolled in a credentialed training program.

Increase in families moving into afforda.indicator: Families newly housed in affordable units
Increase in families moving into afforda.definition: Net new families moving into income-restricted units year over year.

Increase in families purchasing homes.definition: Neighbors closing on a first home purchase.

Foreclosures/evictions prevented.definition: Eviction filings against households in the assistance program.

Up-to-date rental and utility payments.indicator: Households current on rent and utilities
Up-to-date rental and utility payments.definition: Share of supported households with no past-due balance.

Increased income, savings, assets, and/o.definition: Share of financial-coaching participants with higher net worth after a year.

Increased credit scores among residents.definition: Share of coaching participants improving score by 30+ points.

New small businesses created.indicator: New businesses launched on the corridor
New small businesses created.definition: New businesses opened in the commercial corridor.

Students achieving academic benchmarks.definition: Share of students meeting expected annual academic growth.

Increased housing stability.definition: Share of placed households retaining stable housing for a year or more.
Share of pre-redevelopment residents still living in Riverside.

Increased legacy resident retention.definition: Share of relocated legacy households who return to new units.

Generational wealth creation through hom.indicator: Median home equity built by neighbor owners
Generational wealth creation through hom.definition: Median equity accrued by Riverside homeowners assisted by the program.

Revitalized commericial corridor.definition: Share of corridor commercial space that is leased and occupied.

Increased high school graduation.definition: Share of Riverside students graduating within four years.

Increased financial health and economic.definition: Share of households earning above the basic cost-of-living threshold.

A vibrant, socially cohesive community.indicator: Residents reporting strong sense of community
A vibrant, socially cohesive community.definition: Share of residents agreeing they feel connected to neighbors.
